summary.intent: Run an automated Greptile code review loop on PR #730, then fix all flagged issues including security vulnerabilities, async correctness, and file permission hardening.
summary.outcome: All 5 original PR review comments and 2 new Greptile-flagged issues were fixed, with 108 tests passing and clippy clean before pushing.

repo: The RPC response structure uses `res.payload` for data fields, not `res.result` — `res.result?.channels` and `res.result?.auth_url` were both wrong and should be `res.payload?.channels` / `res.payload?.auth_url`.
The project uses `secrecy::Secret<String>` for sensitive tokens and requires manual `Debug` impls that redact with `[REDACTED]`.
OIDC session files must be written with `0o600` permissions (unix-only restriction).
The project requires both `cargo clippy` clean and `biome` lint on JS before committing.
The codebase is in a git worktree under `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/fork-piper/`.

code.finding: Used `res.result?.channels` and `res.result?.auth_url` — both should be `res.payload.*` to match the RPC response shape.
Same `res.result` → `res.payload` field name bug as page-channels.js.

Added `write_session_file` helper that sets `0o600` permissions via `OpenOptionsExt::mode()` — required a `#[cfg(unix)]` import guard to avoid clippy `unused_imports` false positive on non-unix paths.

workflow: Greptile review is triggered via GitHub check runs API, not automatically; must be manually triggered then polled.
Greptile check run output can contain control characters that break `jq` parsing — use Python as a fallback JSON parser.
Greptile comments on the latest commit include both old (already-fixed) and new actionable issues; need to diff by commit to identify truly new findings.
Clippy can emit false-positive `unused_imports` for trait imports (e.g., `OpenOptionsExt`) when the method call is on a foreign type — restructuring the cfg blocks resolves it.
`return Ok(())` as the last expression in a `#[cfg(unix)]` block triggers `clippy::needless_return`.

friction: Greptile check run output contained control characters that caused `jq` to fail silently — required switching to Python for parsing.
Greptile score/confidence value was not surfaced in any accessible API endpoint (check run output, PR body, or inline comments).
Clippy false positive on `OpenOptionsExt` import inside `#[cfg(unix)]` required restructuring the file permissions code multiple times before getting clean.
Greptile re-flagged already-fixed issues from prior commits alongside new findings, requiring manual triage of comment timestamps/commits.
open_items: Greptile confidence score was never retrieved — no known endpoint exposed it during this session.
The polling mechanism for Greptile is ad-hoc bash scripting; a more robust polling loop could be worth standardizing.
PR threads were resolved via CLI after the commit — worth verifying all 5 original + 2 new Greptile threads are marked resolved on GitHub.
